name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America is a national payday and installment lender offering short-term emergency loans at 750+ store locations across ~23 states, also available online.
description_long:
Founded August 11, 1997 and headquartered in Spartanburg, South Carolina, Advance America is one of the largest non-bank short-term lenders in the United States. The company operates under the Purpose Financial umbrella brand and is ultimately owned by Grupo Elektra, a major Latin American banking and retail conglomerate. With 750–800+ physical store locations across approximately 23 states, it also operates under trade names including National Cash Advance, Check Advance, and First American Cash Advance. The company holds state-level non-bank lender licenses in each state it operates in and earned BBB A+ accreditation in September 2024.

Advance America's core product is the payday loan — a short-term advance repaid on the borrower's next payday, typically within 7 to 30 days, carrying APRs of 200% to 700% depending on state law (e.g., a $35 fee on a $300 loan for 14 days in Florida equates to roughly 304% APR). The company also offers installment loans repaid over 3 to 24 months at APRs of 207% to 348%+, with total repayment on an $800 loan potentially reaching $2,221 over 24 months. Additional products include title loans secured by vehicle title (borrower retains use of the vehicle), revolving lines of credit where interest accrues only on the drawn amount, and personal loans for bad-credit borrowers. Products vary significantly by state. Applications are accepted both in-store and online via the portal at online.advanceamerica.net, with approval decisions that can be instant and funds deposited within 24 hours.

Advance America's primary differentiator is scale and accessibility. Few non-bank lenders match its brick-and-mortar footprint, making it a practical option for consumers who prefer face-to-face transactions or lack reliable internet access. The company explicitly markets to borrowers with poor or no credit, requiring no traditional credit score minimum. Unlike many online-only lenders, customers can walk into a store and leave the same day with funds. Its broad product menu — spanning payday, installment, title, and revolving credit products under one brand — distinguishes it from single-product operators in the space.

For borrowers with no other options, Advance America delivers on speed and accessibility. However, the costs are severe: APRs reaching 700% make these among the most expensive financial products legally available to consumers. Despite an A+ BBB rating (which reflects accreditation criteria, not customer satisfaction), the company's BBB customer review score sits at just 1.69 out of 5 from 154 reviews, and it accumulated 302 complaints with the BBB over three years. These products are best used as genuine last resorts for short-term emergencies, not as routine cash flow management tools.

pricing.guarantee_details: No standard money-back guarantee. Partial fee refunds may apply in some states when a loan is repaid early before the due date. Terms depend entirely on state law. Contact (844) 562-6480 or your originating store for specifics.

tiers.name: Payday Loans
features: Short-term loans repaid on next payday (7–30 day terms)
APR typically 200%–700% depending on state
Example: $35 fee on $300 loan for 14 days in Florida = 304% APR
No minimum credit score required
Instant approval decisions; funds within 24 hours
Available in-store and online

tiers.name: Installment Loans
features: Fixed monthly payments over 3–24 month terms
APR typically 207%–348%+
Example: $800 loan totaling approximately $2,221 repaid over 24 months
Higher loan amounts than payday products
Available to borrowers with poor credit
In-store and online application

tiers.name: Title Loans
features: Secured by vehicle title; borrower keeps driving the vehicle
APR varies by state
Available at select store locations
Approval based on vehicle value, not credit score

tiers.name: Lines of Credit
features: Revolving credit line; reusable as balance is repaid
Interest charged only on amount drawn
APR varies by state
Available in select states only

pros: 750–800+ physical store locations across ~23 states — one of the largest payday lender footprints in the US
Both in-store and online applications accepted via online.advanceamerica.net
Approval decisions can be instant with funds deposited within 24 hours
No minimum credit score required — explicitly serves bad-credit and no-credit borrowers
Multiple loan types under one brand: payday, installment, title, and lines of credit
BBB A+ rated and accredited since September 2024
28+ years in operation since 1997 with a national physical presence
cons: Payday loan APRs range from 200% to 700% — among the most expensive legal consumer credit products available
BBB customer review score of only 1.69/5 from 154 reviews despite the A+ accreditation rating
302 BBB complaints filed in the past 3 years, with 74 in the most recent 12 months alone
Only available in approximately 23 states — unavailable in roughly half the country
No confirmed dedicated mobile app as of March 2026
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit who cannot qualify for traditional bank loans or credit cards
People needing emergency cash within 24 hours who have exhausted lower-cost borrowing options
Borrowers who prefer in-person transactions at a physical store location
Underbanked Americans needing a short-term financial bridge between paychecks
diagnosis: Advance America is genuinely best for credit-impaired consumers facing a true financial emergency who need cash in hand within 24 hours and have been turned away by traditional lenders. The critical caveat is cost: payday loan APRs of 200%–700% make these among the most expensive legal consumer financial products available, and the company's 1.69/5 BBB customer review score alongside 302 complaints in three years signals meaningful service-level concerns. These loans should function as a last resort, not a routine financial management tool.
services: Title Loans (vehicle-secured loans, borrower keeps vehicle)
Lines of Credit (revolving, interest on drawn amount only)
Money Services including money orders (at select locations)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
